title: NPC Secrets
description: Something an NPC is hiding — from the party, from other NPCs, or from themselves. Usable as a twist, a vulnerability, or a hook.

entries.result: They witnessed a crime that someone powerful committed and have been silent about it ever since.
The identity they use is not their own. They assumed it years ago and the original owner is still out there.
They are deeply in debt to someone who has not yet called it in.
They were responsible for an accident that killed someone, and the official record blames someone else.
They have a second family in another city that knows nothing about this one.
They informed on a colleague years ago. The colleague went to prison. They still feel it.
They are not who they claim to be professionally. Their credentials are fabricated but their competence is real.
They are slowly stealing from an employer who trusts them completely.
They have been paid to monitor a specific person and report on them regularly.
They are the biological parent of someone they've never acknowledged.
They destroyed evidence that would have exonerated someone. That person is still suffering the consequences.
They are in contact with someone the authorities are looking for.
They have a terminal illness and have told no one.
They accepted a bribe once, and the people who paid it have never forgotten.
They are being blackmailed and have been for years.
They were once a member of a group they now publicly condemn.
They have done something that their religion, community, or family would consider unforgivable.
They are not as loyal as they appear. Their true allegiance is elsewhere.
They are the one who started a rumor that ruined someone's life. The rumor was false.
They have a child they gave up and have been trying to find for years.
They once let someone die when they could have saved them, for reasons of self-interest.
Their successful business was built on money obtained through illegal means that have since been cleaned.
They know where something valuable is hidden and have been waiting for the right time to retrieve it.
They are not the person they appear to be. Their whole life in this city is a constructed cover.
They have been feeding information to a rival of their employer without the employer's knowledge.
They once killed someone and was never suspected. They've convinced themselves it was necessary.
They are in a relationship their family, community, or employer would never accept.
They have a significant addiction that they manage carefully enough that no one has noticed.
They sabotaged a competitor in a way that ended that person's career.
They know that the official version of a local historical event is wrong, and they know why.
They have been skimming from community funds in small amounts for a long time.
They are related to someone they regularly speak about with contempt.
They have faked their own documents at least once and may need to again.
They know who is responsible for an unsolved crime but won't say because they'd have to explain how they know.
They have a private vice that contradicts their public persona.
They are in contact with someone they claim not to know.
They sold something that wasn't theirs to sell.
They have been lying about their background to maintain a position they genuinely earned through other means.
They once fled from a place where people needed them.
They hold a belief completely at odds with everything their life publicly represents.
They have been collecting information on the people around them for years, just in case.
They were the source of a betrayal that affected many people. Only two people know this. One is dead.
They know what happened to a missing person that everyone else is looking for.
They owe their current position to a powerful patron whose favor they secured through flattery and minor deception.
They have a significant past achievement that would embarrass the people they now associate with.
They are responsible for a small ongoing injustice that they've convinced themselves is unavoidable.
They made a promise they can no longer keep and haven't told the person they made it to.
They know the location of something that multiple powerful interests are looking for.
They have been reporting on a friend to someone who doesn't wish that friend well.
They are carrying out instructions left by someone dead, and the instructions are becoming a problem.
They are not the person who is supposed to be doing this job. The real person left. They stepped in. It worked. It's been years.
They covered up evidence of a problem that has since grown larger.
They are in love with someone they claim to regard with complete professional neutrality.
They have a standing arrangement with a criminal element that they entered into for good reasons and have never been able to exit.
They have stolen from someone they love, just once, under great pressure. The theft was never discovered.
They know their current partner's infidelity and has said nothing, for reasons that make sense to them.
They are keeping something safe that they were given against their will, and that keeping it has cost them.
They once had a chance to prevent a disaster and didn't take it for reasons that still embarrass them.
They have made preparations to leave everything and everyone behind at a moment's notice.
They are aware of a fraud being carried out by someone they can't report without implicating themselves.
They are far more capable of violence than their manner suggests.
They have a connection to a long-dead figure of power that no one living knows about.
They have been paying a specific person to stay silent about a specific thing for years.
They have access to information about the people around them that they gathered without permission.
They are hiding an inherited secret that belongs to their family, not to them personally.
They played a role in a public tragedy and have never been implicated.
They have a professional skill they've never disclosed because revealing it would raise questions they can't answer.
They have been approached to betray the people around them and have not yet decided what to do.
They know that a rumor being spread about a third party is true, but confirming it would harm them too.
They are the last surviving member of something that everyone believes to be extinct or dissolved.
They have been lying about their health to maintain their position.
They have destroyed evidence in their own favor, legally but not ethically.
They are waiting for a specific signal to take an action that will change everything around them.
They know that a document everyone relies on contains an error they introduced deliberately.
They are responsible for a rupture in a relationship between two other people they care about.
They have been given information they weren't supposed to receive and can't unknow it.
They gave a false testimony once that was never challenged and has become accepted history.
They have a powerful enemy who doesn't know they exist. Yet.
They secretly agree with the thing they spend the most energy arguing against.
They are responsible for the disappearance of something everyone is still looking for.
They have been replaced at their job by an impostor — and know it — and haven't reported it.
They have a long-standing relationship with a person who is officially their enemy.
They know that someone currently in a position of authority obtained that position through fraud.
They were complicit in an injustice that they have since worked to make right in every way except acknowledgment.
They have an illegitimate claim to something they currently hold through legitimate means.
They have been systematically undermining someone they appear to support.
They have a document in their possession that would upend several people's lives if it were read.
They once let someone take blame that was partly or entirely theirs.
They know the location of a person who is in hiding.
They have been living in the aftermath of something that the people around them don't know happened.
They have a grudge against someone in the party's orbit and have been waiting for an opportunity.
They are far wealthier than they appear, for reasons they have not disclosed.
They are far poorer than they appear, and have been maintaining appearances through increasingly creative means.
They know that a public institution they represent is hiding something significant.
They have been quietly working against the interests of someone they appear to serve faithfully.
They have taken something that was not theirs and built their life on it.
They have a past that, if known, would make their current social position impossible.
They are doing this — all of this — for one person who does not know the extent of what they've done.
They are exactly who they appear to be, which no one believes, and the disbelief is starting to matter.
They have no secrets. This is itself the secret, because everyone assumes they must.
They have been told something in confidence that they desperately want to act on.
They have a recurring breakdown they manage in private and fear someone will eventually notice.
They are carrying on a years-long correspondence with someone using a false name.
They know about a plot against someone they should warn, but warning them would expose how they know.
They have been living under a promise they made under duress that they've never found a way to break.
They know the identity of a person who has been trying to stay anonymous for very good reasons.
They have something that belongs to a powerful person, acquired accidentally, and haven't found a way to return it.
They have been quietly giving resources to a cause that their associates would consider treason.
They were the one who reported the party's activities to someone who shouldn't have that information.
They have been planning to leave for months and haven't told the people who depend on them.
They made a decision that cost lives, under incomplete information, and were never held accountable.
They know who the father or mother of a locally important person is, and it isn't who is named.
They have been manipulating a situation behind the scenes with results that serve them but harm others.
They know that a death ruled natural was not.
They have been in contact with a person everyone else believes is dead.
They have documents in their possession that were never supposed to survive a particular event.
They carry the knowledge of something that would start a conflict if it became widely known.
They have been tested without knowing it and have failed in ways they haven't found out about yet.
They know that something officially dangerous is actually harmless, and something dismissed is not.
They are not what the party thinks they are. The role they're playing is not the role they've been assigned.
They have recently come into possession of information that changes everything about their situation.
They have been surveilling the party since before the party knew they existed.
They were approached to betray someone and said no, but haven't told that person they were approached.
They have a professional relationship that has become personal and is now affecting their judgment.
They have done something that their most respected mentor would consider deeply wrong.
They know who is responsible for a recurring crime that officials have stopped trying to solve.
They are working for more than one party in this situation, and none of them know about the others.
They have been deliberately underperforming for years to avoid a promotion they don't want.
They are the only witness to something that could change the outcome of an ongoing situation.
They have been maintaining a correspondence under a false name for longer than they can remember starting.
They know the real reason why a longstanding local feud started, and the real reason makes both sides look bad.
They are innocent of something they've confessed to, for reasons that made sense at the time.
They made a private agreement that was supposed to expire and hasn't.
They have a complete set of records they were supposed to destroy and didn't.
They received information through means they're not willing to disclose, and the information is accurate.
They have a skill that, if known, would mark them as something they've been denying being.
They were present at something that everyone thought went smoothly, and they know it almost didn't.
They know the location of a cache of valuables that someone else believes was lost.
They have been acting on orders that they've been told to deny receiving.
They have a loyalty to someone in the opposition that predates all their other loyalties.
They signed something they shouldn't have signed and it's binding.
They know a truth about the party that the party doesn't know about themselves.
They have been sabotaging the party's mission, not out of malice, but out of fear of what happens if it succeeds.
They are protecting someone whose guilt is not in question.
They are the one who set the current situation in motion, unintentionally, long before the party arrived.
They have been a step ahead of everyone because they wrote the plan everyone is reacting to.
They want to help the party but helping them would require revealing something that would destroy the NPC's life.
They were the original target of something that hit someone else, and they've never said so.
They know how this ends. They've seen the pattern before. They're not saying anything because no one would listen.
They are the author of an anonymous work that has had significant effects on the world around them.
They have been running a long con on everyone around them, including people who think they're in on it.
They know that the threat everyone is preparing for is not the real threat.
They have already decided to betray someone and are simply waiting for the right moment.
They are the only person who knows that a central assumption of the current situation is wrong.
They have been loyal to a cause that has since been revealed as corrupt, and they are still loyal.
They know how to stop what's happening and won't share it until certain conditions are met.
They are the third party in a conflict that everyone thinks is between two parties.
They were the one who delivered the party to this situation, and the delivery was not accidental.
They know that the person the party is trying to save doesn't want to be saved.
They have information that would make the party's current mission impossible to care about.
They are not in danger. The danger was staged. The reasons are complicated.
They arranged for the party to be involved in this and regret it now.
They know that the villain the party is pursuing is doing something the party would not want stopped.
They are an observer for an outside interest and have been filing reports since the beginning.
They are responsible for something the party is trying to fix, but they believe it needed to happen.
They have been waiting for someone like the party for a long time, for reasons the party won't like.
They are the one who hired the people who are now hunting the party.
They have already done what the party is trying to do, and the consequences were not what anyone expected.
They know where the story ends. They've been trying to change it. They haven't been able to.
They are already dead. What the party has been speaking to is something wearing their shape.
They have been controlled or influenced by something external, and they know it, and they can't stop it.
They know the party's true purpose here, which is not the purpose the party believes they have.
They have made a bargain on behalf of everyone around them without consulting anyone.
They know that the thing the party is protecting is not worth protecting. They have said nothing.
They are not the person they appear to be in any sense. Every detail the party has been given about them is wrong.
They are the one who will betray the party, and they know it, and they're trying not to.
They are the one who is supposed to stop the party, and they've decided not to.
They are a fragment of something larger — the part that was sent to deal with the party.
They know what is at the center of everything and will only share it with someone they trust completely.
They have already won. What the party is doing is the last step of a plan that succeeded months ago.
They are waiting to see what the party does before deciding which side to be on.
They are protecting a truth that will destroy the party if they learn it.
They have made a sacrifice that no one knows about, and it is what made everything else possible.
They know that the person the party is working for is the one responsible for everything.
They are the only innocent person in a situation where everyone looks guilty.
They have information they will share, but only if the party is willing to pay the price it costs.
They have been through this before. Different names, different faces. Same shape. They survived last time.
They are the cause of the party's current problem and believe they were right to set it in motion.
They know the party's real names, histories, and capabilities, and they haven't said so.
They have been running every faction in this conflict. The conflict serves them.
They are trying to prevent the party from succeeding, not because they want to, but because success will be worse.
They are testing the party. The test is not over. The party has not passed.
They know the truth the party is looking for. They have decided not to tell it, for reasons they won't explain, because explaining would cost more than concealing.
They have been paid to let the party succeed. The people who paid are not on the party's side.
They are the person the party has been looking for, and they have been standing in front of them from the beginning.
They know the question the party should have been asking instead of the one they are.
They have already betrayed the party and is trying to make up for it before it becomes obvious.
They have been working against a force that no one else knows exists, alone, for a long time.
They are the one who sent the party here. Everything that has happened since is what they intended.
They love someone in the party. They have since the first meeting. This has compromised everything they've done since.
